hero.question: Will Michigan win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 34.5%, the crowd is pricing Michigan as a roughly 11-to-1 longshot, which feels about right for a program that hasn't won it all since 1989 and lacks elite lottery talent in the 2025-26 rotation. The real tell here is volume: $1.2M in 24 hours on a market that won't resolve for 14+ months suggests sharp money is either locking in value on a sleeper or hedging overconfidence, so watch whether this drifts toward 40% (team talent exceeds expectations) or collapses below 30% (roster gaps become obvious by March).

daily_take.headline: Michigan's Tournament Odds Flatline While Arizona Creeps Up. Smart Money Knows Why.
daily_take.deck: Arizona just grabbed 2.2 points to match Michigan at 34.3%, but the real story is the gap between what these prices should be. Michigan's resume looks stronger heading into 2026, yet the market treats them as interchangeable. Sharp bettors should ask: is Arizona's momentum a signal of new information, or just noise from recent games? The answer determines whether you fade one or both.
